prs.title: Fix/5539 zod omit pick
prs.body:
## Summary

Add a regression type test for `createInsertSchema()` so `.omit()` and `.pick()` accept standard `{ key: true }` masks without TS errors.

## Changes

- `drizzle-zod/tests/sqlite.test.ts`
  - Added compile-time checks for:
    - `omit({ id: true, createdAt: true })`
    - `pick({ id: true, name: true })`

## Validation

- `pnpm --filter drizzle-zod test:types` 

Closes #5539

prs.title: Fix registry 404 and docs preview rendering
prs.body:
# Fix legacy registry 404 and docs preview reliability

## Summary
This PR fixes a docs/registry regression in the v4 app where legacy registry style URLs returned 404 for some components (notably combobox), and docs previews could appear blank for lazy-loaded examples.

## Problem
- Legacy registry URL requests like `/r/styles/new-york/combobox.json` failed.
- Radix combobox docs referenced a mismatched style key.
- Lazy demo previews in docs did not always render reliably.

## Root Cause
- Registry JSON for impacted components exists under `new-york-v4`, but some consumers still request `new-york` (or `default`).
- The combobox docs page referenced `base-nova` instead of `radix-nova`.
- Preview tabs rendered lazy content without a suspense boundary.

## What Changed
### 1) Backward-compatible registry rewrites
Updated Next.js rewrites to map legacy style paths to canonical v4 style paths:
- `/r/styles/new-york/:path*` -> `/r/styles/new-york-v4/:path*`
- `/r/styles/default/:path*` -> `/r/styles/new-york-v4/:path*`

File changed:
- `apps/v4/next.config.mjs`

### 2) Corrected combobox docs style references
Replaced combobox docs style references with the correct radix style key.

File changed:
- `apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/combobox.mdx`

### 3) Improved preview rendering resilience
Wrapped preview component rendering in `React.Suspense` (with fallback) for both LTR and RTL branches.

File changed:
- `apps/v4/components/component-preview-tabs.tsx`

## Impact
- Legacy registry URLs now resolve to existing canonical assets.
- Combobox docs examples and source sections point to correct style assets.
- Docs example previews are more reliable for lazy-loaded examples.

## Validation
- Confirmed canonical registry artifacts exist for affected components:
  - `public/r/styles/new-york-v4/combobox.json`
  - `public/r/styles/new-york-v4/popover.json`
  - `public/r/styles/new-york-v4/tooltip.json`
- Confirmed docs style references in combobox page now use `radix-nova`.
- Confirmed suspense wrappers exist in preview tabs for both render branches.

prs.title: Fix boosted scatter reset zoom y-axis
prs.body:
Fixed #24386, reset zoom on boosted scatter now correctly restores full axis extremes so points are no longer missing after zoom reset.

### What changed
- Updated boost scatter data processing to recalculate and restore full min/max axis extremes after reset zoom in [ts/Extensions/Boost/BoostSeries.ts](ts/Extensions/Boost/BoostSeries.ts).
- Added a regression unit test for zoom + reset behavior on boosted scatter in [samples/unit-tests/boost/scatter-zoom-reset-y-axis/demo.js](samples/unit-tests/boost/scatter-zoom-reset-y-axis/demo.js).

### Tests

prs.title: Fix {@const} staleness in callbacks for prop-derived values
prs.body:
## Fix: {@const} inside {#each} now updates with prop-derived values in callbacks ### Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following - [x] It's really useful if your PR references an issue where it is discussed ahead of time. In many cases, features are absent for a reason. For large changes, please create an RFC: https://github.com/sveltejs/rfcs - [x] Prefix your PR title with `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, or `docs:`. - [x] This message body should clearly illustrate what problems it solves. - [x] Ideally, include a test that fails without this PR but passes with it. - [x] If this PR changes code within `packages/svelte/src`, add a changeset (`npx changeset`). ### Tests and linting - [x] Run the tests with `pnpm test` and lint the project with `pnpm lint` --- ## Related Issue Fixes #17992 ## Problem In legacy mode, `{@const}` declarations would become stale when a prop reference appeared only inside array method callbacks like `.find()`, `.filter()`, or `.some()`. **Example of the bug:** ```svelte export let selectedItemId = null; {#each groups as group} {@const selectedItem = group.items.find((item) => item.id === selectedItemId)}

{selectedItem?.seats}

{/each}

prs.title: Support jest.config.mts
prs.body:
## Summary

This PR adds support for `jest.config.mts` as a valid Jest configuration file.

Jest currently supports `jest.config.ts`, `jest.config.js`, `jest.config.mjs`, `jest.config.cjs`, `jest.config.cts`, and `jest.config.json`, but does not support the TypeScript ESM extension `.mts`. This creates friction in ESM-first TypeScript projects where `.mts` is used to explicitly define ESM behavior independent of the `package.json` `type` field.

This change resolves the issue by:
- Adding `.mts` to config extension constants and implicit config resolution order.
- Updating config loading to treat `.mts` as TypeScript and load it via ESM.
- Updating the module loading helper to use dynamic import for `.mts`.
- Adding and updating unit tests and e2e tests for config discovery and loading.
- Adding fixtures for `.mts` config scenarios.
- Updating CLI extension validation test expectations.
- Updating documentation (current and versioned) to include `.mts` as a supported config file.

## Test plan

- Attempted to run targeted tests for all modified areas.

Result:
- Test execution could not be completed in this environment due to missing dependencies.
- Error observed: `Couldn't find the node_modules state file - running an install might help.`

Additional validation:
- No editor or type errors were reported for the modified files.

CI:
- Full test validation is expected to run in CI for this branch.

Note:
- A `CHANGELOG.md` entry will be added before merge, as per contribution guidelines.

prs.title: issue-384 bridge
prs.body:
## Describe your changes

Implemented the neural-lam side of issue #384 by adding a bridge-oriented graph creation flow in `create_graph` that can delegate graph construction + tensor-on-disk serialization to weather-model-graphs, while preserving legacy behavior.

### Summary of the changes
- Added archetype-oriented CLI options:
  - `--archetype keisler|graphcast|hierarchical`
  - `--graph_name`
  - `--output_dir`
  - `--mesh_node_distance`
  - `--max_num_levels`
  - `--level_refinement_factor`
  - `--backend auto|wmg|legacy`
- Added optional WMG-backed execution path with safe fallback to the legacy in-repo generator.
- Preserved backward compatibility via deprecated aliases:
  - `--name`
  - `--levels`
  - `--hierarchical`
- Added CLI tests for:
  - archetype argument forwarding
  - deprecated alias behavior
  - conflict handling between `--name` and `--graph_name`
- Added bridge planning/future-work document:
  - `docs/wmg_neural_lam_bridge_plan.md`
- Updated README graph-creation usage examples to reflect the new CLI.

### Motivation and context
This addresses the planning and implementation direction discussed in #384:
- keep the CLI thin and orchestration-focused
- move graph construction + neural-lam serialization responsibilities toward weather-model-graphs
- maintain compatibility with existing neural-lam users during transition

### Dependencies
- No mandatory new dependency for existing users.
- Optional dependency: `weather-model-graphs` (only required when using `--backend wmg` or when `--backend auto` resolves to WMG).

## Issue Link

Related to #384

## Type of change

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [x] 📖 Documentation (Addition or improvements to documentation)

## Checklist before requesting a review

- [ ] My branch is up-to-date with the target branch - if not update your fork with the changes from the target branch (use `pull` with `--rebase` option if possible).
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] For any new/modified functions/classes I have added docstrings that clearly describe its purpose, expected inputs and returned values
- [x] I have placed in-line comments to clarify the intent of any hard-to-understand passages of my code
- [x] I have updated the [README](README.md) to cover introduced code changes
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [x] I have given the PR a name that clearly describes the change, written in imperative form ([context](https://www.gitkraken.com/learn/git/best-practices/git-commit-message#using-imperative-verb-form)).
- [ ] I have requested a reviewer and an assignee (assignee is responsible for merging). This applies only if you have write access to the repo, otherwise feel free to tag a maintainer to add a reviewer and assignee.

## Checklist for reviewers

Each PR comes with its own improvements and flaws. The reviewer should check the following:
- [ ] the code is readable
- [ ] the code is well tested
- [ ] the code is documented (including return types and parameters)
- [ ] the code is easy to maintain

## Author checklist after completed review

- [ ] I have added a line to the CHANGELOG describing this change, in a section reflecting type of change (add section where missing):
  - *added*: when you have added new functionality
  - *changed*: when default behaviour of the code has been changed
  - *fixes*: when your contribution fixes a bug
  - *maintenance*: when your contribution is relates to repo maintenance, e.g. CI/CD or documentation

## Checklist for assignee

- [ ] PR is up to date with the base branch
- [ ] the tests pass
- [ ] (if the PR is not just maintenance/bugfix) the PR is assigned to the next milestone. If it is not, propose it for a future milestone.
- [ ] author has added an entry to the changelog (and designated the change as *added*, *changed*, *fixed* or *maintenance*)
- Once the PR is ready to be merged, squash commits and merge the PR.

prs.title: fix: improve microphone permission guidance

## Proposed Changes

Fixes #14895  
- replaces the Chrome-only microphone-permission link with dedicated help links for Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Safari.  
- updates the `AudioCaptureDialog` permission-denied state to show those links so users on any browser can quickly re-enable mic access which would work on all devices as all brower docs are responsive.

Tagging: @ohcnetwork/care-fe-code-reviewers

- [ ] Update [product documentation](https://docs.ohc.network).

prs.title: feat: add skeleton loaders for improved UX
prs.body: ## Related Tickets & Documents Fixes: #3293 ## Description Added skeleton loaders across the blog website to improve user experience during content loading. This provides visual feedback to users while data is being fetched, making the application feel more responsive and reducing perceived loading times. The implementation uses shadcn/ui's skeleton component as the base and creates custom skeleton loaders for different content types (hero posts, post cards, post grids, and top blogs). ### Changes - Added shadcn/ui skeleton component to the project - Created 4 custom skeleton components (HeroPostSkeleton, PostCardSkeleton, PostGridSkeleton, TopBlogsSkeleton) - Integrated loading states on homepage, community page, and technology page - Added proper loading state management with conditional rendering - Updated component structure to support skeleton loaders ## Type of Change - [x] New feature (change that adds functionality) - [x] UI improvement (visual or design changes) ## Testing - Tested skeleton loaders appear correctly during initial page load - Verified smooth transition from skeleton to actual content - Tested responsive behavior across mobile, tablet, and desktop viewports - Confirmed no console errors or warnings - Verified all pages (home, community, technology) display appropriate skeletons - Tested with slow network throttling to ensure skeletons remain visible during loading ## Demo ### Before ### After ## Environment and Dependencies - **New Dependencies:** - `@radix-ui/react-slot` (added by shadcn/ui) - `class-variance-authority` (for component variants) - `tailwind-merge` (for className utilities) - `lucide-react` (for icons, if not already present) - **Configuration Changes:** - Added `components.json` for shadcn/ui configuration - Added `lib/utils.ts` for cn() utility function - No environment variable changes required ## Checklist - [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project - [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code - [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation - [ ] I have added corresponding tests - [x] I have run the build command to ensure there are no build errors - [x] My changes have been tested across relevant browsers/devices - [x] For UI changes, I've included visual evidence of my changes

prs.title: channel labels: Use channelTopicLabelSpan for consistent alignment.
prs.body: ### Overview This pull request updates the message list app bar to use the canonical `channelTopicLabelSpan` helper for rendering a channel icon and name. The motivation for this change is a visible vertical misalignment between the channel icon and the channel name in the message list app bar. Other UI locations were reviewed but left unchanged where alignment was already visually correct. The goal of this PR is to fix that specific alignment issue while avoiding unnecessary visual or spacing changes elsewhere. --- ### Summary of Changes This PR includes the following updates, structured into separate commits: 1. **Message list app bar title** - Replaced the custom `Row` based implementation with `channelTopicLabelSpan`. - Fixes the vertical alignment of the channel icon relative to the channel name. 2. **Widget tests** - Updated affected widget tests to match the widget tree produced by `channelTopicLabelSpan`. Other locations that were initially explored, such as channel rows and combined feed headers, were intentionally left unchanged after review, as their existing alignment was already correct. --- ### Screenshots (Before & After) --- ## **Message list App Bar** | Before | After | |--------|-------| | | | --- ## **Combined Feed** | Before | After | |--------|-------| | | | --- ### Open Questions / Notes There are no open questions. This PR focuses on a single visible alignment issue and avoids unrelated visual changes. The unrelated failing local tests (`content_test.dart`, `katex_test.dart`) were due to a filesystem path encoding issue on my machine. --- ### Cross links - Issue: #1906 - Related PR: #1877 --- ### Self review Checklist - [x] Commits are organized into minimal coherent changes. - [x] No commit fixes a mistake from a previous commit. - [x] UI changes are limited to a single visible alignment issue. - [x] Tests affected by widget structure changes updated. - [x] Added before and after screenshots for affected screens. - [x] Manually reviewed logic and visual correctness.

prs.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing a pull request! Here are a few tips to help you:

1. If this is your first contribution, make sure you've read the Contribution Checklist https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute/getting-started/contribution-checklist/
2. Read our blog post about "Submitting Great PRs" https://developers.mattermost.com/blog/2019-01-24-submitting-great-prs
3. Take a look at other repository specific documentation at https://developers.mattermost.com/contribute
-->

#### Summary
This PR fixes an issue where opening a permalink initially scrolls correctly to the highlighted post, but after the virtualized post list finishes rendering (images, embeds, file attachments, lazy loaded posts), the scroll position shifts. As a result, the highlighted message may end up partially or completely outside the viewport.  
The fix ensures that the highlighted message remains centered or fully visible even after the list re-renders.

#### Ticket Link
Fixes https://github.com/mattermost/mattermost/issues/34462

#### Screenshots
N/A  no UI visual changes.

#### Release Note
```release-note
Fixed an issue where highlighted messages opened via permalink would shift out of view after additional content finished rendering.
```

issues.title: Bug: Microphone permission help link does not adapt to user’s browser
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
When microphone permission is denied, the UI displays a help link that redirects users to a Chrome-specific support page. This causes confusion for users on non-Chrome browsers (Safari, Firefox, Edge, mobile browsers), as the instructions may not match their browser or platform.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Open the application in any browser.
2. Trigger audio recording without granting microphone permission.
3. Deny the microphone permission when prompted.
4. Observe the permission-denied UI.
5. Click on the “Allow microphone access” / help link.

**Expected behavior**

1. The help link should redirect users to browser-appropriate, vendor-maintained documentation for enabling microphone permissions, ensuring:

  - Correct instructions for the user’s browser
  - No browser-specific assumptions in the UI
  
**Screenshots**
N/A (UI text and link behavior only)

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: macOS / Windows / Linux
- Browser: Firefox / Safari / Edge
-  Version: Latest stable

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**

- Device: Android / iPhone
- OS: Android / iOS
- Browser: Firefox / Safari / Edge
- Version: Latest stable

**Additional context**
The current implementation uses a single Chrome help link, which is misleading for Safari, Firefox, in both desktop  and mobile users. This is problematic mostly for patient side.

---

**Describe your solution clearly:**
Provide a detailed explanation of your proposed solution, including your approach, key implementation steps, and relevant examples or references. Mention any dependencies, assumptions, or risks you foresee that might affect your timeline or implementation.

**Expected Timeline:**

**Additional Context:**
Include any other relevant context, links, screenshots, or resources that support your proposed solution.

issues.title: Filter button hidden off-screen on Appointments Board view
issues.body: **Describe the bug** Filter button is not visible on the Appointments page (Board view) without horizontal scrolling. The filter functionality is hidden off-screen on the default view, making it difficult for users to discover and access filtering options. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Login as a doctor or staff member 2. Navigate to `/facility/{facilityId}/appointments` 3. Observe the default Board view that loads 4. Notice that the Filter button (with filter icon) is not visible in the viewport 5. Scroll horizontally to the right 6. See the Filter button appears in the far right corner near the "Non Fulfilled" column **Expected behavior** The Filter button should be visible in the default viewport without requiring horizontal scrolling.
